# GC-01: Audit Trail & Compliance Reporting

**Track:** Governance Controls (`GC`) · **Priority:** MUST (mandatory) · **Status:** draft

Log every control execution with timestamp, actor, outcome, and supporting evidence. Produce DORA examination artefacts on demand. The audit trail must be immutable and tamper-evident.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 0 | Cross-Cutting | Mandatory |

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Fully automated |
| **Agent does** | Execute all control logging and produce audit artefacts automatically. |
| **Human does** | Review audit reports on demand; present artefacts during regulatory examinations. |

## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Control execution log | log_entry | Timestamped, attributed record of every control execution with outcome and evidence references. | 7 years (DORA Art. 8(6)) |
| Regulatory examination pack | report | On-demand compilation of all audit artefacts mapped to regulatory obligations. | 7 years (DORA Art. 8(6)) |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_change |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | — |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | block |
| **Escalate to** | Compliance Officer |

If audit logging fails, the associated control execution is blocked until logging is restored.

## Dependencies

None

## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 8(6) | Documentation and reporting of ICT risk management activities |
| DORA | Art. 5(2) | Management body accountability for ICT risk framework |
| EU AI Act | Art. 12 | Record-keeping and logging requirements for high-risk AI systems |
| EU AI Act | Art. 17(1)(d) | Quality management documentation requirements |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Governance & Compliance · **Owner role:** Compliance Officer
